desc: Onomichi is a hillside port city famed for temple walks and as the western start of the Shimanami Kaidō cycling route across the Seto Inland Sea.
hls: Iconic Shimanami Kaidō bike route gateway
Temple Walk overlooking the Seto Inland Sea
Retro alleyways, cafés, and ramen culture
Easy rail links from Hiroshima and Osaka

todo.name: Temple Walk route
todo.desc: Follow the hillside temple path with city-and-sea viewpoints.

todo.name: Shimanami Kaidō day cycle
todo.desc: Ride at least one island segment using bike-rental return stations.

todo.name: Cat Alley + old shopping arcades
todo.desc: Explore narrow lanes, galleries, and restored storefronts.

todo.name: Onomichi ramen crawl
todo.desc: Compare soy-based local ramen shops near the station and waterfront.

todo.name: Senkōji ropeway and park
todo.desc: Ride up for dusk views and descend by hillside lanes.

todo.name: Mukaishima ferry hop
todo.desc: Use short ferries to see shipyard and island neighborhoods.

wifi.note: Reliable in station-area hotels and cafés; older ryokan may have weaker Wi-Fi.

prac.fltNote: Access is typically via Tokyo or Osaka long-haul arrivals, then shinkansen/local rail; UK nonstop capacity to Kansai and Haneda peaks in summer and year-end holiday periods.
prac.currency: Japan local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Cycling route bridges can be windy—check advisories before committing to long island segments.
Many cafés and galleries close midweek, so avoid packing all key stops into one weekday.
Typhoon season can suspend ferries and ropeway operations with short notice.

sweet: Apr-May and Oct-Nov for comfortable cycling weather and clearer Seto views.
